tasks.description: Add spam domain blacklist enforcement to invoice creation
tasks.context: Add SPAM_DOMAINS constant array in backend/src/api/invoices-create.ts. Check merchant.email domain against blacklist before invoice creation. Return 403 with clear error if matched. Log blocked attempts.

issues.description: Using console.log for logging instead of a proper logging framework - should use structured logger for production
SPAM_DOMAINS array could be externalized to config for easier maintenance without code changes
Missing JSDoc on main createInvoice function
Uses console.log for structured event logging rather than the project's centralized logger abstraction (e.g. pino or Winston).

strengths: Proper TypeScript with strict typing and no 'any' usage
Comprehensive Zod validation schema already in place
Clean separation of concerns with helper functions
Appropriate HTTP status codes (403 for forbidden, 400 for validation)
Clear error messages for end users
Good domain extraction logic with null safety
Maintains existing functionality while adding new feature
Follows existing code patterns and architecture
Proper async/await usage with error handling
Zod schema ensures full input validation in strict mode
Clear extraction and blacklist check of email domains
Proper HTTP status codes and error messages for invalid input and blocked domains
JSDoc comments on helper functions improve maintainability

tasks.description: Implement task auto-decomposition validator in agent orchestrator
tasks.context: Add validateTaskComplexity() function in backend/src/services/ledger/enforcement.ts. Check task context length, file count in deliverables. If context >600 chars OR files >1, reject with TASK_TOO_COMPLEX error. Return decomposition hints.

tasks.description: Wire complexity validator into invoice creation flow
tasks.context: Import validateTaskComplexity in backend/src/api/invoices-create.ts. Call validator before processing invoice creation. If validation fails, return 400 with decomposition instructions. Preserve existing invoice logic.

tasks.description: Extract QUALITY-002 webhook retry logic to isolated function
tasks.context: In backend/src/services/webhook/dispatch.ts, create retryWebhookWithBackoff() function handling exponential backoff, max retries 3, delays [1s,5s,15s]. Move existing retry logic into this function. Add retry count logging.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (25/100) + Codex (20/100). File is incomplete with syntax error and missing critical functionality | The generated file is incomplete/truncated and does not implement the required retryWebhookWithBackoff() function as specified. Core functionality is missing and the code cannot compile.

issues.description: File is truncated - ends with incomplete 'logg' statement causing syntax error
Missing the required retryWebhookWithBackoff() function - task spec requires moving existing retry logic into this named function
File appears to have lost existing functionality - only contains deliverWithRetry() but no main dispatch logic or webhook registration handling
Custom logger implementation instead of using established project logging - should use existing logger from project
Missing error handling for the final failure case after all retries exhausted
The file is abruptly truncated (‘logg…’) and missing its closing braces and return path—code will not compile.
The spec mandates a new retryWebhookWithBackoff() function encapsulating the exponential backoff logic. That function is not present.

tasks.description: Extract QUALITY-003 chain validation to reusable validator
tasks.context: In backend/src/lib/chain-validator.ts, create validateChainCompatibility() function accepting chainId and address. Check chain exists in registry, validate address format. Return {valid: boolean, error?: string}. Extract from inline checks.

review.summary: CEO resolved conflict (Codex approved, Claude rejected): REJECT — Claude identified critical blockers that would cause runtime failures. The code imports from `./chain-registry` which doesn't exist, making this completely non-functional. Missing tests also violates our "test everything" company rule. While Codex praised the structure, shipping code with m

issues.description: Imports getSupportedChains and getChain from './chain-registry' but this module doesn't exist in the codebase - will cause runtime errors
No test file provided - violates requirement for Jest unit tests with >80% coverage intent
Re-exports getSupportedChains and getChain from non-existent module, creating additional import failures
Error handling throws generic Error instead of using custom error types as per project standards
Missing input sanitization beyond basic trim() - should validate against injection patterns

strengths: Safe error rethrow for unexpected failures
No use of any or insecure patterns

tasks.description: Extract QUALITY-004 rate limit config to centralized constants
tasks.context: In backend/src/middleware/rate-limiter.ts, create RATE_LIMIT_TIERS constant object with {free: {requests: 100, window: '1h'}, paid: {requests: 1000, window: '1h'}}. Replace hardcoded values with tier lookups.

review.summary: Dual-rejected: Claude (25/100) + Codex (30/100). Code is incomplete - file cuts off mid-function and lacks the actual rate limiting implementation. While the structure and types are well-designed, this is not functional code. | The submitted rate-limiter file is incomplete and contains syntax errors; the createRateLimiter implementation is truncated and will not compile or function.

issues.description: File is incomplete - cuts off in the middle of createRateLimiter function at line with incomplete template literal
Missing the actual rate limiting logic - no implementation of request counting, window tracking, or HTTP 429 responses
In-memory store (Map) will not work in production with multiple instances - needs Redis or shared storage
Over-engineered for the simple task spec - added unnecessary interfaces, validation, and complexity beyond what was requested
The createRateLimiter function is cut off mid-statement, missing closing parentheses/braces and the rest of the implementation, leading to invalid TypeScript syntax.
